text: Close the recursive loop: auto-propose improvement aims from recurring system limitations
description: The "identify improvements → apply improvements" half of b03ad58e. When a friction bucket dominates the recent error log (e.g. busy-timeout ×N), the loop should propose a concrete aim in the wrapped-agents package to address that limitation at the source, rather than only retrying through it. Builds on the friction summarizer. Decide: auto-create the aim vs. surface a suggestion for human confirmation (avoid graph spam).

status.comment: Capture+feedback exists. Implemented auto-propose directive in friction summary: when retry ceiling dominates, the summary now includes explicit **AUTO-PROPOSE ACTION** with create_aim tool call and suggested text for fixing in supervisor-state.ts. Added test that passes. LLM/agent loop will now auto-propose the improvement aim instead of just 'consider'.

reflection: Investigated via MCP get_aim_context and code grep/read in wrapped-agents/common/src (session-memory.ts, watchdog-service.ts, supervisor-state.ts, kennel references in session-server). Confirmed no prior programmatic auto-propose of create_aim from friction (still relied on 'consider' text). The retry ceiling is normalized in normalizeFrictionReason and surfaces in summarizeRecentFriction and injected to instruct context. Edited summarizeRecentFriction to append direct AUTO-PROPOSE instruction + suggested aim when 'retry ceiling reached' is top bucket. Updated test to assert the new text and create_aim directive. Test passes (tsx --test). This makes the loop programmatically drive proposal of the fix aim via the friction block the agent sees. High effectiveness: turns passive into actionable. Addresses the missing half of the recursive self-improvement for wrapped-agents supervisor friction like retry ceiling.
